data.body:
## Post-delivery review — fixes in commit d68ff494

Adversarial Opus review of the implementation (0ecf0198). Verdict
important-fixes-required; one important defect found and resolved, plus
hardening + the test-coverage gaps it flagged.

- [important] Reducer cache version was not bumped despite `updated_at` being a
  new projected field. A pre-P1.1 `.cache.json` (matching dir-hash) would be
  served verbatim → updated_at=None for untouched tickets post-upgrade. Fixed:
  _REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION 2→3 (folds into the dir hash, invalidates stale caches).
  Regression test added (stale-v2-cache invalidation).
- [minor] Bare `not:`/`-` is now a parser no-op (was an empty substring term that
  matched everything and emptied negated result sets).
- Test gaps closed: `--sort updated` e2e (the only sort dim using derived state),
  `list`/`ready` sort e2e, priority range (`n..m`,`*..n`) e2e, parent-alias
  resolution e2e.
- [doc] reads.py (930 LOC) moved into the >800 offender table in architecture.md.

Confirmed-clean by the review (no change needed): Jira-sync safety (reconciler
never whole-dict-serializes reduced state), SNAPSHOT event-byte parity (strip is
non-mutating), apply_ticket_filters refactor is behavior-preserving, sort
stability/None-last. Remaining items were nits/by-design (case-sensitive
predicate values; known-`field:` text reinterpreted as predicate — GitHub-style,
documented in help).
